_comment_1: EXAMPLE — not a finished security policy.
_comment_2: Copy to .claude/settings.json in your project, adapt paths and tools to your stack.
_comment_3: permissions.deny ALWAYS wins, including under sandbox auto-allow. Use deny for destructive operations, NOT ask.
_comment_4: See https://github.com/web-werkstatt/claude-code-guardrails for the destructive-bash hook this pairs with.

_notes.deny_vs_ask: Use deny for destructive operations (rm, git rm, etc.). ask is bypassed under sandbox auto-allow. See claude-code-guardrails docs.
_notes.hooks: PreToolUse Bash-hook is the hard layer. Stop-hook is for sync/reminder logic, must be idempotent.
_notes.adapt_paths: Replace design-source, design-templates, archive paths with your project's protected paths.
